TEXT.citation: Early, R. J., & Whitehorn, J. (2003). One hundred Paiwan texts. Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University.
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @book{100paiwantexts, author = {Robert Early and John Whitehorn}, title = {One Hundred Paiwan Texts}, year = {2003}, publisher = {Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University}}
TEXT.source: ti Sapayas katua ɫa Qavulung (Tjakuvukuvuɫ village, p.207)

S.FORM: ka sitsuayan izua ti Sapayas; vaik a sema Velikiɫ.
S.PHON: ka siʦuajan izua ti sapajas; vaik a səma vəɭikiʎ.
S.FORM: ka sicuayan izua ti Sapayas; vaik a sema Velikilj.
S.PHON: ka siʦuajan izua ti sapajas; vaik a səma vəɣikiʎ.
S.TRANSL: Once upon a time someone called Sapayas went to Velikiɫ.
He met people of the Qavulung family,

S.FORM: qau maɫavar tua kadjunangan i Velikiɫ.
S.PHON: qau maʎavar tua kaɉunaŋan i vəɭikiʎ.
S.FORM: qau maljavar tua kadjunangan i Velikilj.
S.PHON: qau maʎavaR tua kaɉunaŋan i vəɣikiʎ.
S.TRANSL: and they discussed the land at Velikiɫ.

S.FORM: "muri a niaken tsu a kadjunangan," aya ti Sapayas.
S.PHON: "muri a niakən ʦu a kaɉunaŋan," aja ti sapajas.
S.FORM: "muri a niaken cu a kadjunangan," aya ti Sapayas.
S.PHON: "muRi a niakən ʦu a kaɉunaŋan," aja ti sapajas.
S.TRANSL: “This land will be mine,” said Sapayas.

S.FORM: manu "a niamen tsu a kadjunangan," aya marivu tiamadju.
S.PHON: manu "a niamən ʦu a kaɉunaŋan," aja marivu tiamaɉu.
S.FORM: manu "a niamen cu a kadjunangan," aya marivu tiamadju.
S.PHON: manu "a niamən ʦu a kaɉunaŋan," aja maRivu tiamaɉu.
S.TRANSL: But they disagreed; “This land is ours,” they said.

S.FORM: "metsevung anga a maɫavar nu tjaiviliɫ," aya, sa vaik a mavaday.
S.PHON: "məʦəvuŋ aŋa a maʎavar nu caiviɭiʎ," aja, sa vaik a mavadaj.
S.FORM: "mecevung anga a maljavar nu tjaivililj," aya, sa vaik a mavaday.
S.PHON: "məʦəvuŋ aŋa a maʎavaR nu ʦaiviɣiʎ," aja, sa vaik a mavadaj.
S.TRANSL: “We’ll meet to discuss it later,” they said, and went their separate ways.

S.FORM: tsuatsuay anga pagaɫu, qau metsevung sema zua i Pinaqaquɫatan.
S.PHON: ʦuaʦuaj aŋa pagaʎu, qau məʦəvuŋ səma zua i pinaqaquʎatan.
S.FORM: cuacuay anga pagalju, qau mecevung sema zua i Pinaqaquljatan.
S.PHON: ʦuaʦuaj aŋa pagaʎu, qau məʦəvuŋ səma zua i pinaqaquʎatan.
S.TRANSL: After quite a little time they met at Pinaqaquɫatan.

S.FORM: matsidiɫ ti Sapayas; a se Vulavulat izua mapuɫat.
S.PHON: maʦidiʎ ti sapajas; a sə vuɭavuɭat izua mapuʎat.
S.FORM: macidilj ti Sapayas; a se Vulavulat izua mapuljat.
S.PHON: maʦidiʎ ti sapajas; a sə vuɣavuɣat izua mapuʎat.
S.TRANSL: Sapayas was on his own, but all the Vulavulat people were there.
“Let’s discuss the land,” said Sapayas.
But the Qavulung family said, “Come on, let’s fight!”
Sapayas was shot at.
“Don’t shoot!
Let’s talk,” said Sapayas.
They didn’t listen, and kept on shooting.

S.FORM: a nu panaqen, ipuq ti Sapayas; ini kadjameq.
S.PHON: a nu panaqən, ipuq ti sapajas; ini kaɉaməq.
S.FORM: a nu panaqen, ipuq ti Sapayas; ini kadjameq.
S.PHON: a nu panaqən, ipuq ti sapajas; ini kaɉaməq.
S.TRANSL: When he was shot at, Sapayas lay down, and wasn’t hit.
“Don’t shoot!
Let’s talk,” said Sapayas.

S.FORM: ini langeda ɫa Qavulung; kamayan a panaq.
S.PHON: ini ɭaŋəda ʎa qavuɭuŋ; kamajan a panaq.
S.FORM: ini langeda lja Qavulung; kamayan a panaq.
S.PHON: ini ɣaŋəda ʎa qavuɣuŋ; kamajan a panaq.
S.TRANSL: The Qavulung family didn’t listen, and kept on shooting.
“If you challenge me, just wait a bit.

S.FORM: temaɫiw anan aken tua ku tseqeɫap," aya ti Sapayas.
S.PHON: təmaʎiw anan akən tua ku ʦəqəʎap," aja ti sapajas.
S.FORM: temaljiw anan aken tua ku ceqeljap," aya ti Sapayas.
S.PHON: təmaʎiw anan akən tua ku ʦəqəʎap," aja ti sapajas.
S.TRANSL: I’ll just sharpen my sword,” said Sapayas.
Sapayas sharpened it;
and fire burst out all over the level land.
The fire reached Riɫuriɫuan.
“Hey, no!
This is no good.
We won’t be able to stand this.

S.FORM: tja kaviladay," aya ɫa Qavulung, sa kavilad.
S.PHON: ca kaviɭadaj," aja ʎa qavuɭuŋ, sa kaviɭad.
S.FORM: tja kaviladay," aya lja Qavulung, sa kavilad.
S.PHON: ʦa kaviɣadaj," aja ʎa qavuɣuŋ, sa kaviɣad.
S.TRANSL: We’ll run away,” said the Qavulung family, and they ran away.
“That’s enough.

S.FORM: nia sisaluan anga a kadjunangan," ayain ni Qavulung ti Sapayas.
S.PHON: nia sisaɭuan aŋa a kaɉunaŋan," ajain ni qavuɭuŋ ti sapajas.
S.FORM: nia sisaluan anga a kadjunangan," ayain ni Qavulung ti Sapayas.
S.PHON: nia sisaɣuan aŋa a kaɉunaŋan," ajain ni qavuɣuŋ ti sapajas.
S.TRANSL: We concede the land,” said Qavulung to Sapayas.
So that was the end (or So he was victorious).

S.FORM: saka ni Sapayas a kadjunangan tutsu azazua i Velikiɫ, aya a tjautsikel.
S.PHON: saka ni sapajas a kaɉunaŋan tuʦu azazua i vəɭikiʎ, aja a cauʦikəɭ.
S.FORM: saka ni Sapayas a kadjunangan tucu azazua i Velikilj, aya a tjaucikel.
S.PHON: saka ni sapajas a kaɉunaŋan tuʦu azazua i vəɣikiʎ, aja a ʦauʦikəɣ.
S.TRANSL: And the land at Velikiɫ belongs to Sapayas now, according to the story.
